name: wifipumpkin3
title: wifipumpkin3
summary: Powerful framework for rogue access point attack
description:
Powerful framework for rogue access point attack
This package contains a powerful framework for rogue access point attack,
written in Python, that allow and offer to security researchers, red teamers
and reverse engineers to mount a wireless network to conduct a
man-in-the-middle attack.

commands.name: wp3
commands.description: usage: wp3 [-h] [-i INTERFACE] [-iNet INTERFACE_NET] [-s SESSION] [-p PULP]

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use captiveflask because Powerful framework for rogue access point attack.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use captiveflask as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `captiveflask [options] [args]` as documented upstream for captiveflask.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair captiveflask with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `captiveflask --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
